categories.description: Customer-facing. Sent to the customer when they submit a refund request via POST /refunds. Confirms receipt + summarises the requested amount + reason + next steps (approval pending / auto-approved). Transactional — cannot-opt-out per US CAN-SPAM 4(a)(3)(a) (required for the refund-flow disclosure).

Admin-facing. Sent to every user holding `refunds.approve` permission when a refund triggers requires_approval=true (amount / staleness threshold OR always-approve reason). Signed magic link deep-links to approve/reject UI. Segregation of duties — initiator's user_id is filtered out of the recipient list.

Customer-facing. Sent to the customer when refund transitions to `approved` (either auto-approved OR approver clicked approve). Confirms the refund is queued for provider dispatch. Estimated delivery is 5-10 days for card refunds, faster for other rails.

Customer + admin. Sent when refund transitions to `processing` (provider dispatched, awaiting async confirmation). Communicates the estimated_delivery_at window when provider-communicated. Explains the 5-10 day card refund delay to prevent support escalations.

Customer-facing. Sent when refund transitions to `succeeded` (provider confirmed terminal success). For card refunds, this notification is 'money should have landed by now' since we can't detect exact card-refund delivery. Admin variant carries the credit_note_id + transaction_id for ledger audit.

Customer + admin. Sent when refund transitions to `failed` (provider rejected non-recoverably). Includes support-contact CTA + a next-step alternative (bank transfer / check via manual finance path). Admin variant carries the provider_error_code for triage.
Customer-facing. Sent when approver clicks reject. Includes the rejection_reason (256 char admin-provided) + support-contact CTA. Customer's original payment remains unchanged; no money moves.
